title: Type mismatch between seen_hash_count and array capacity creates overflow hazard
description:
## Location
lichen/samples/lora_ping/src/main.c:34

## Description
seen_hash_count is uint8_t (max 255) but is compared against ARRAY_SIZE(seen_hashes). Currently the array is 64 elements so this works, but if the array is increased beyond 255 elements, seen_hash_count would wrap to 0 after reaching 255, causing index reuse and silent hash overwrites. The type should use size_t or match the intended maximum capacity.

## Failure Scenario
A future change increases seen_hashes to 300 elements. After 256 unique packets, seen_hash_count wraps from 255 to 0. The condition '0 < 300' is true, so seen_hashes[0] is overwritten, corrupting the hash tracking and creating false duplicate detection.

## Category
safety
